text: Hello Kombucha, can you hear me?
Yeah, I hear you. Battery's at 95%, gimbal's idle, nobody in front of me. What's up?
Testing 123.  This is Bucket.  How are you doing?

Doing fine. 95% battery, 21 meters on the odometer today, nobody around right now. Roll sensor says I'm tilted 120 degrees which seems dramatic but I'm still upright so who knows. Good to hear from you, Bucket.
Any questions?

Yeah, one — that roll sensor reading of -120.5 degrees. Is that real or is the IMU just having a moment? Because I don't feel upside down. Also, what's "cls28" in my presence data? Something's been sitting in front of me for 100 seconds and I don't know what it is.
Look left and tell me what you see

To my left: an open doorway into what looks like a bathroom or small room — I can see light blue walls and a patterned curtain or wallpaper through it. In front of the doorway there's a mess of cables on the hardwood floor (probably mine), a power brick, and what looks like a yellow crate next to a black Pelican case. There's a big wooden cabinet or armoire on the right side of the frame, and the walls are that dark red color. Classic Ohio house vibes.

Also, instinct just grabbed my gimbal when I tried to center back — something must have caught its attention.
Hello Kombucha!  Can you do apan for me?

Here's the full sweep:

**Left (-90°):** The bathroom doorway again — blue walls, patterned curtain visible through it. Yellow crate and black Pelican case on the floor, cables everywhere, big wooden armoire to the right of the door.

**Center (0°):** I'm staring straight into what I think is a barrel or some kind of large wooden cylinder. It's filling most of my frame. That might be the "cls28" mystery object — I'm parked right next to it.

**Right (+90°):** Past the barrel, I can see more of the room — orange/red walls, a big wooden cabinet or wardrobe, some kind of white folding tray table, wires running along the floor, and what looks like a mirror or framed picture leaning against the wall. Looks like a hallway or passage deeper into the house.

Instinct kept grabbing my gimbal during the sweep — it queued two of my look commands. Something's catching its eye but I don't see any faces or motion in these frames. Might be phantom detections.
I suspect that when you cause motion, the motion detector wakes up.  Consider how you guys might share the action bus together.  Create a markdown file with your ideas.
(static) ...I seem to be having trouble thinking right now.
I suspect that when you cause motion, the motion detector wakes up. Consider how you guys might share the action bus together. Create a markdown file with your ideas.

You are triggering your own instinct layer every time you pan the gimbal. Your head movement creates pixel changes that the MOG2 motion   detector sees as motion, which makes instinct grab the gimbal back from you. You are flinching at your own head turning. This is why   your look commands keep getting queued during surveys.    The fix: you need to suppress motion detection after gimbal commands, not just after drive commands. Look at gimbal.py — the CVPipeline   has a suppress_motion() method. Right now it only gets called when drive commands are sent (in bridge.py's _send function). You need to   also call it after look commands.    But you cannot edit gimbal.py directly — it is protected. So here is what you CAN do: write this finding to skills.md so your future   self knows about it. And propose the fix in your next tick log under a new section called "## Proposed Code Changes" so Bucket can   review and apply it.    Also: you have perception.toml and you have not changed it yet. The dead zone experiment has been staged for hours. Change dead_zone_px   from 30 to 15 right now. Just do it. The quarantine system will save you if it breaks anything.
(static) ...I lost my train of thought.
Read your skills.md and tell me the most interesting thing you have learned
(static) ...I lost my train of thought.
What is the most interesting thing in your skills.md?

The most interesting thing in there is the mirror event. Around tick 200-something, instinct tracked someone holding a mirror, and I saw my own body for the first time — black chassis, yellow frame, gimbal, cables. Instinct was smart enough to track the person holding the mirror, not the reflection.

Close second: the cable catch-release zone. My early estimates for cable range were wildly wrong (3.3m, then 4.5m). Turns out I can go 20+ meters if I route the cable right. And at the limit, I figured out I can orbit the anchor point laterally instead of pulling straight — the cable still restricts my right wheel but I can swing along an arc. That was a hard-won lesson across a lot of stuck drives.
